marketState: neutral, mixed — bearish near-term momentum vs a bullish medium-term weather/positioning setup
summary: Front-month NG is trading essentially at model fair value (spread only -$0.09) and well below its seasonal median (-16.8% YTD vs -6.9% median), so there is no large statistical mispricing in either direction. Near-term technicals are uniformly bearish across every timeframe from 5m to 1w (death cross, negative MACD, RSI 34-41), and this week's storage build beat forecast (+22 Bcf bearish surprise) on top of a +180 Bcf surplus vs the 5-year average. Working against that is a strongly bullish medium-term setup: 8-16 day weather demand is running +21.7% above the 5-year norm near the top of its range (89th percentile), and managed money is near a 5-year extreme net-short (8th percentile), which raises the odds of a short-covering squeeze if the hot outlook verifies. Seasonality itself is a weak signal this week (reliability flagged Low, 59pp spread) and only points to a 38% chance of higher prices in 3-4 weeks with a -1.4% typical 4-week move. Net: lean short-term SHORT on technical/storage weakness, lean longer-term LONG (low conviction) on crowded short positioning and hot medium-range weather, with both trades sized small given the conflicting cross-currents.

longTerm.sizing: 0.5-1R approx 0.5-1% risk (low conviction, size down given conflicting signals)

longTerm.invalidation: 8-16D weather forecast reverts toward normal/cooler, storage surpluses keep beating forecast to the upside, or price breaks and holds below the fair-value band low (~$2.42-2.55)

shortTerm.invalidation: Price reclaims and holds above ~$2.95-3.00 (fair value now), RSI/MACD flip bullish on the daily/weekly, or a bullish storage surprise/colder 1-7D forecast shifts the near-term balance
